== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]]
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near [[:en:Byakugō-ji|Byakugō-ji]], merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in [[:en:Hachijō|Hachijō, Nara]].<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Sakura Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Fukuzumi, Tenri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ([[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]], ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]], ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ([[:en:Saigyō|Saigyō]], ''[[:en:Sankashū|Sankashū]]'')

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11382931]] Wikidata name: Saho River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Osaka Prefecture's Ibaraki City river|Ibaraki River}} {{Infobox River | name = Saho River | image = [[File:佐保川の桜１.jpg|300px|Saho River, photographed March 2007]] | image_caption = View of the Saho River from Ōmiya Bridge in Hōren-chō, Nara City, facing Mount Wakakusa | basin_countries = Japan | river_system = [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] system | river_type = {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} | length = 19 km | source1 = {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nara Prefecture) | source1_elevation = 460 m | mouth = [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] (Nara Prefecture) | basin_size = 129 km² | discharge1 = -- | region = Nara Prefecture }}
The '''Saho River''' (佐保川, ''Sahogawa'') is a {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} and a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]]. It flows through the northern areas of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Geography == [[File:川路桜２.jpg|thumb|Cherry blossoms along the riverbanks, said to have been developed during the tenure of {{ill|Kawaji Saneomi|ja|川路聖謨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaji Saneomi|1=Kawaji Toshiakira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3532613}} as Nara Magistrate]] [[File:Saho River, Daian-ji, Nara.jpg|thumb|Saho River flowing through Koi-no-Kubo in Nara City during autumn]] The river originates near [[:en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest|Mount Kasuga Primeval Forest]] between Mount Hana and Mount Yoshi, at {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Hase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />
At its headwaters, the source features a waterfall known as Uguisu Falls, which is 10 meters in height.<ref name="yamato">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamato-shinkin.co.jp/news/detail/pdf/pdf_0059.pdf|title=Our Connection to the Yamato River No.9: Saho River|publisher=Yamato Shinkin Bank|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www3.pref.nara.jp/park/secure/2833/kasugayamagenshirin_guide.pdf|title=Guide to Kasuga Primeval Forest|publisher=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-01}}</ref>
In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], as per the principles of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]].<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>
== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]]
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near [[:en:Byakugō-ji|Byakugō-ji]], merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in [[:en:Hachijō|Hachijō, Nara]].<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Sakura Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Fukuzumi, Tenri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ([[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]], ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]], ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ([[:en:Saigyō|Saigyō]], ''[[:en:Sankashū|Sankashū]]'')

It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Hase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />
At its headwaters, the source features a waterfall known as Uguisu Falls, which is 10 meters in height.<ref name="yamato">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamato-shinkin.co.jp/news/detail/pdf/pdf_0059.pdf|title=Our Connection to the Yamato River No.9: Saho River|publisher=Yamato Shinkin Bank|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www3.pref.nara.jp/park/secure/2833/kasugayamagenshirin_guide.pdf|title=Guide to Kasuga Primeval Forest|publisher=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-01}}</ref>
In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], as per the principles of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]].<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>
== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]]
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near [[:en:Byakugō-ji|Byakugō-ji]], merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in [[:en:Hachijō|Hachijō, Nara]].<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Sakura Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Fukuzumi, Tenri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ([[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]], ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]], ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ([[:en:Saigyō|Saigyō]], ''[[:en:Sankashū|Sankashū]]'')

The '''Saho River''' (佐保川, ''Sahogawa'') is a {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} and a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]]. It flows through the northern areas of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Geography == [[File:川路桜２.jpg|thumb|Cherry blossoms along the riverbanks, said to have been developed during the tenure of {{ill|Kawaji Saneomi|ja|川路聖謨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaji Saneomi|1=Kawaji Toshiakira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3532613}} as Nara Magistrate]] [[File:Saho River, Daian-ji, Nara.jpg|thumb|Saho River flowing through Koi-no-Kubo in Nara City during autumn]] The river originates near [[:en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest|Mount Kasuga Primeval Forest]] between Mount Hana and Mount Yoshi, at {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Hase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />
At its headwaters, the source features a waterfall known as Uguisu Falls, which is 10 meters in height.<ref name="yamato">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamato-shinkin.co.jp/news/detail/pdf/pdf_0059.pdf|title=Our Connection to the Yamato River No.9: Saho River|publisher=Yamato Shinkin Bank|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www3.pref.nara.jp/park/secure/2833/kasugayamagenshirin_guide.pdf|title=Guide to Kasuga Primeval Forest|publisher=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-01}}</ref>
In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], as per the principles of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]].<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>
== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]]
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near [[:en:Byakugō-ji|Byakugō-ji]], merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in [[:en:Hachijō|Hachijō, Nara]].<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Sakura Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Fukuzumi, Tenri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ([[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]], ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]], ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ([[:en:Saigyō|Saigyō]], ''[[:en:Sankashū|Sankashū]]'')

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11382931]] Wikidata name: Saho River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Osaka Prefecture's Ibaraki City river|Ibaraki River}} {{Infobox River | name = Saho River | image = [[File:佐保川の桜１.jpg|300px|Saho River, photographed March 2007]] | image_caption = View of the Saho River from Ōmiya Bridge in Hōren-chō, Nara City, facing Mount Wakakusa | basin_countries = Japan | river_system = [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] system | river_type = {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} | length = 19 km | source1 = {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nara Prefecture) | source1_elevation = 460 m | mouth = [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] (Nara Prefecture) | basin_size = 129 km² | discharge1 = -- | region = Nara Prefecture }}
The '''Saho River''' (佐保川, ''Sahogawa'') is a {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} and a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]]. It flows through the northern areas of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Geography == [[File:川路桜２.jpg|thumb|Cherry blossoms along the riverbanks, said to have been developed during the tenure of {{ill|Kawaji Saneomi|ja|川路聖謨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaji Saneomi|1=Kawaji Toshiakira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3532613}} as Nara Magistrate]] [[File:Saho River, Daian-ji, Nara.jpg|thumb|Saho River flowing through Koi-no-Kubo in Nara City during autumn]] The river originates near [[:en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest|Mount Kasuga Primeval Forest]] between Mount Hana and Mount Yoshi, at {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Hase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />
At its headwaters, the source features a waterfall known as Uguisu Falls, which is 10 meters in height.<ref name="yamato">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamato-shinkin.co.jp/news/detail/pdf/pdf_0059.pdf|title=Our Connection to the Yamato River No.9: Saho River|publisher=Yamato Shinkin Bank|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www3.pref.nara.jp/park/secure/2833/kasugayamagenshirin_guide.pdf|title=Guide to Kasuga Primeval Forest|publisher=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-01}}</ref>
In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], as per the principles of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]].<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>
== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]]
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near [[:en:Byakugō-ji|Byakugō-ji]], merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in [[:en:Hachijō|Hachijō, Nara]].<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Sakura Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Fukuzumi, Tenri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ([[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]], ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]], ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ([[:en:Saigyō|Saigyō]], ''[[:en:Sankashū|Sankashū]]'')

== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]]
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near [[:en:Byakugō-ji|Byakugō-ji]], merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in [[:en:Hachijō|Hachijō, Nara]].<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Sakura Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Fukuzumi, Tenri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ([[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]], ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]], ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ([[:en:Saigyō|Saigyō]], ''[[:en:Sankashū|Sankashū]]'')

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Saho River}} {{wikidata link|Q11382931}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Rivers of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato River System]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11382931]] Wikidata name: Saho River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Osaka Prefecture's Ibaraki City river|Ibaraki River}} {{Infobox River | name = Saho River | image = [[File:佐保川の桜１.jpg|300px|Saho River, photographed March 2007]] | image_caption = View of the Saho River from Ōmiya Bridge in Hōren-chō, Nara City, facing Mount Wakakusa | basin_countries = Japan | river_system = [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] system | river_type = {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} | length = 19 km | source1 = {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nara Prefecture) | source1_elevation = 460 m | mouth = [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] (Nara Prefecture) | basin_size = 129 km² | discharge1 = -- | region = Nara Prefecture }}
The '''Saho River''' (佐保川, ''Sahogawa'') is a {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} and a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]]. It flows through the northern areas of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Geography == [[File:川路桜２.jpg|thumb|Cherry blossoms along the riverbanks, said to have been developed during the tenure of {{ill|Kawaji Saneomi|ja|川路聖謨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaji Saneomi|1=Kawaji Toshiakira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3532613}} as Nara Magistrate]] [[File:Saho River, Daian-ji, Nara.jpg|thumb|Saho River flowing through Koi-no-Kubo in Nara City during autumn]] The river originates near [[:en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest|Mount Kasuga Primeval Forest]] between Mount Hana and Mount Yoshi, at {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Hase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />
At its headwaters, the source features a waterfall known as Uguisu Falls, which is 10 meters in height.<ref name="yamato">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamato-shinkin.co.jp/news/detail/pdf/pdf_0059.pdf|title=Our Connection to the Yamato River No.9: Saho River|publisher=Yamato Shinkin Bank|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www3.pref.nara.jp/park/secure/2833/kasugayamagenshirin_guide.pdf|title=Guide to Kasuga Primeval Forest|publisher=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-01}}</ref>
In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], as per the principles of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]].<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>

It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Hase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />
At its headwaters, the source features a waterfall known as Uguisu Falls, which is 10 meters in height.<ref name="yamato">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamato-shinkin.co.jp/news/detail/pdf/pdf_0059.pdf|title=Our Connection to the Yamato River No.9: Saho River|publisher=Yamato Shinkin Bank|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www3.pref.nara.jp/park/secure/2833/kasugayamagenshirin_guide.pdf|title=Guide to Kasuga Primeval Forest|publisher=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-01}}</ref>
In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], as per the principles of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]].<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>
== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]]
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near [[:en:Byakugō-ji|Byakugō-ji]], merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in [[:en:Hachijō|Hachijō, Nara]].<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Sakura Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Fukuzumi, Tenri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ([[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume]], ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]], ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ([[:en:Saigyō|Saigyō]], ''[[:en:Sankashū|Sankashū]]'')

The '''Saho River''' (佐保川, ''Sahogawa'') is a {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} and a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]]. It flows through the northern areas of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokōriyama]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Geography == [[File:川路桜２.jpg|thumb|Cherry blossoms along the riverbanks, said to have been developed during the tenure of {{ill|Kawaji Saneomi|ja|川路聖謨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaji Saneomi|1=Kawaji Toshiakira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3532613}} as Nara Magistrate]] [[File:Saho River, Daian-ji, Nara.jpg|thumb|Saho River flowing through Koi-no-Kubo in Nara City during autumn]] The river originates near [[:en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest|Mount Kasuga Primeval Forest]] between Mount Hana and Mount Yoshi, at {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Hase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />

However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11382931]] Wikidata name: Saho River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Osaka Prefecture's Ibaraki City river|Ibaraki River}} {{Infobox River | name = Saho River | image = [[File:佐保川の桜１.jpg|300px|Saho River, photographed March 2007]] | image_caption = View of the Saho River from Ōmiya Bridge in Hōren-chō, Nara City, facing Mount Wakakusa | basin_countries = Japan | river_system = {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} system | river_type = {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} | length = 19 km | source1 = {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nara Prefecture) | source1_elevation = 460 m | mouth = {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} (Nara Prefecture) | basin_size = 129 km² | discharge1 = -- | region = Nara Prefecture }}
The '''Saho River''' (佐保川, ''Sahogawa'') is a {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} and a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}}. It flows through the northern areas of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}} and {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokōriyama}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Geography == [[File:川路桜２.jpg|thumb|Cherry blossoms along the riverbanks, said to have been developed during the tenure of {{ill|Kawaji Saneomi|ja|川路聖謨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaji Saneomi|1=Kawaji Toshiakira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3532613}} as Nara Magistrate]] [[File:Saho River, Daian-ji, Nara.jpg|thumb|Saho River flowing through Koi-no-Kubo in Nara City during autumn]] The river originates near {{ill|Kasugayama Primeval Forest|en|Kasugayama Primeval Forest|WD=Q5367406|lt=Mount Kasuga Primeval Forest}} between Mount Hana and Mount Yoshi, at {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}. It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Hase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />
At its headwaters, the source features a waterfall known as Uguisu Falls, which is 10 meters in height.<ref name="yamato">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamato-shinkin.co.jp/news/detail/pdf/pdf_0059.pdf|title=Our Connection to the Yamato River No.9: Saho River|publisher=Yamato Shinkin Bank|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www3.pref.nara.jp/park/secure/2833/kasugayamagenshirin_guide.pdf|title=Guide to Kasuga Primeval Forest|publisher=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-01}}</ref>
In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital {{ill|Heijō-kyō|en|Heijō-kyō|WD=Q862509|lt=Heijō-kyō}}, as per the principles of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}.<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>
== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokōriyama}}
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near {{ill|Byakugō-ji|en|Byakugō-ji|WD=Q1018450|lt=Byakugō-ji}}, merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in {{ill|Hachijō|en|Hachijō|WD=Q401889|lt=Hachijō, Nara}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from {{ill|Shiroyama|en|Shiroyama|WD=Q7498962|lt=Shiroyama}} in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}, and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Sakura Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Fukuzumi, Tenri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ({{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume}}, ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ({{ill|Saigyō|en|Saigyō|WD=Q224689|lt=Saigyō}}, ''{{ill|Sankashū|en|Sankashū|WD=Q11467762|lt=Sankashū}}'')
== References == {{Reflist}}

(Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ({{ill|Saigyō|en|Saigyō|WD=Q224689|lt=Saigyō}}, ''{{ill|Sankashū|en|Sankashū|WD=Q11467762|lt=Sankashū}}'')

In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital {{ill|Heijō-kyō|en|Heijō-kyō|WD=Q862509|lt=Heijō-kyō}}, as per the principles of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}.<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>
== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokōriyama}}
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near {{ill|Byakugō-ji|en|Byakugō-ji|WD=Q1018450|lt=Byakugō-ji}}, merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in {{ill|Hachijō|en|Hachijō|WD=Q401889|lt=Hachijō, Nara}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from {{ill|Shiroyama|en|Shiroyama|WD=Q7498962|lt=Shiroyama}} in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}, and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Sakura Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Fukuzumi, Tenri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ({{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume}}, ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ({{ill|Saigyō|en|Saigyō|WD=Q224689|lt=Saigyō}}, ''{{ill|Sankashū|en|Sankashū|WD=Q11467762|lt=Sankashū}}'')

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saho River]][[ja:佐保川]]
{{Otheruses||Osaka Prefecture's Ibaraki City river|Ibaraki River}} {{Infobox River | name = Saho River | image = [[File:佐保川の桜１.jpg|300px|Saho River, photographed March 2007]] | image_caption = View of the Saho River from Ōmiya Bridge in Hōren-chō, Nara City, facing Mount Wakakusa | basin_countries = Japan | river_system = {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} system | river_type = {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} | length = 19 km | source1 = {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nara Prefecture) | source1_elevation = 460 m | mouth = {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} (Nara Prefecture) | basin_size = 129 km² | discharge1 = -- | region = Nara Prefecture }}
The '''Saho River''' (佐保川, ''Sahogawa'') is a {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Class A river system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} and a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}}. It flows through the northern areas of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}} and {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokōriyama}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Geography == [[File:川路桜２.jpg|thumb|Cherry blossoms along the riverbanks, said to have been developed during the tenure of {{ill|Kawaji Saneomi|ja|川路聖謨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaji Saneomi|1=Kawaji Toshiakira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3532613}} as Nara Magistrate]] [[File:Saho River, Daian-ji, Nara.jpg|thumb|Saho River flowing through Koi-no-Kubo in Nara City during autumn]] The river originates near {{ill|Kasugayama Primeval Forest|en|Kasugayama Primeval Forest|WD=Q5367406|lt=Mount Kasuga Primeval Forest}} between Mount Hana and Mount Yoshi, at {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}. It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Hase River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />
At its headwaters, the source features a waterfall known as Uguisu Falls, which is 10 meters in height.<ref name="yamato">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamato-shinkin.co.jp/news/detail/pdf/pdf_0059.pdf|title=Our Connection to the Yamato River No.9: Saho River|publisher=Yamato Shinkin Bank|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www3.pref.nara.jp/park/secure/2833/kasugayamagenshirin_guide.pdf|title=Guide to Kasuga Primeval Forest|publisher=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-01}}</ref>
In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital {{ill|Heijō-kyō|en|Heijō-kyō|WD=Q862509|lt=Heijō-kyō}}, as per the principles of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}.<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>
== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokōriyama}}
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near {{ill|Byakugō-ji|en|Byakugō-ji|WD=Q1018450|lt=Byakugō-ji}}, merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in {{ill|Hachijō|en|Hachijō|WD=Q401889|lt=Hachijō, Nara}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from {{ill|Shiroyama|en|Shiroyama|WD=Q7498962|lt=Shiroyama}} in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}, and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Sakura Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Fukuzumi, Tenri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ({{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume}}, ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ({{ill|Saigyō|en|Saigyō|WD=Q224689|lt=Saigyō}}, ''{{ill|Sankashū|en|Sankashū|WD=Q11467762|lt=Sankashū}}'')

{{Otheruses||Osaka Prefecture's Ibaraki City river|Ibaraki River}} {{Infobox River | name = Saho River | image = [[File:佐保川の桜１.jpg|300px|Saho River, photographed March 2007]] | image_caption = View of the Saho River from Ōmiya Bridge in Hōren-chō, Nara City, facing Mount Wakakusa | basin_countries = Japan | river_system = {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} system | river_type = {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Classification of rivers in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} | length = 19 km | source1 = {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nara Prefecture) | source1_elevation = 460 m | mouth = {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} (Nara Prefecture) | basin_size = 129 km² | discharge1 = -- | region = Nara Prefecture }}
The '''Saho River''' (佐保川, ''Sahogawa'') is a {{ill|Class A river|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Class A river|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Classification of rivers in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2908753}} and a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}}. It flows through the northern areas of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} and {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokōriyama|1=Yamatokōriyama}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Geography == [[File:川路桜２.jpg|thumb|Cherry blossoms along the riverbanks, said to have been developed during the tenure of {{ill|Kawaji Saneomi|ja|川路聖謨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaji Saneomi|1=Kawaji Toshiakira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3532613}} as Nara Magistrate]] [[File:Saho River, Daian-ji, Nara.jpg|thumb|Saho River flowing through Koi-no-Kubo in Nara City during autumn]] The river originates near {{ill|Kasugayama Primeval Forest|en|Kasugayama Primeval Forest|WD=Q5367406|lt=Mount Kasuga Primeval Forest|1=Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} between Mount Hana and Mount Yoshi, at {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}. It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Q17503129|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />
At its headwaters, the source features a waterfall known as Uguisu Falls, which is 10 meters in height.<ref name="yamato">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamato-shinkin.co.jp/news/detail/pdf/pdf_0059.pdf|title=Our Connection to the Yamato River No.9: Saho River|publisher=Yamato Shinkin Bank|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www3.pref.nara.jp/park/secure/2833/kasugayamagenshirin_guide.pdf|title=Guide to Kasuga Primeval Forest|publisher=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-01}}</ref>
In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital {{ill|Heijō-kyō|en|Heijō-kyō|WD=Q862509|lt=Heijō-kyō|1=Heijō-kyō}}, as per the principles of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}.<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>
== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokōriyama|1=Yamatokōriyama}}
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near {{ill|Byakugō-ji|en|Byakugō-ji|WD=Q1018450|lt=Byakugō-ji|1=Byakugō-ji}}, merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in {{ill|Hachijō|en|Hachijō|WD=Q401889|lt=Hachijō, Nara|1=Hachijō}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from {{ill|Shiroyama|en|Shiroyama|WD=Q7498962|lt=Shiroyama|1=Shiroyama}} in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Q11537986|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Q11591777|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ({{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|1=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume}}, ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ({{ill|Saigyō|en|Saigyō|WD=Q224689|lt=Saigyō|1=Saigyō}}, ''{{ill|Sankashū|en|Sankashū|WD=Q11467762|lt=Sankashū|1=Sankashū}}'')

== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokōriyama|1=Yamatokōriyama}}
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near {{ill|Byakugō-ji|en|Byakugō-ji|WD=Q1018450|lt=Byakugō-ji|1=Byakugō-ji}}, merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in {{ill|Hachijō|en|Hachijō|WD=Q401889|lt=Hachijō, Nara|1=Hachijō}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from {{ill|Shiroyama|en|Shiroyama|WD=Q7498962|lt=Shiroyama|1=Shiroyama}} in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Q11537986|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Q11591777|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />

({{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|1=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume}}, ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' == Geography == [[File:川路桜２.jpg|thumb|Cherry blossoms along the riverbanks, said to have been developed during the tenure of {{ill|Kawaji Saneomi|ja|川路聖謨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaji Saneomi|1=Kawaji Toshiakira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3532613}} as Nara Magistrate]] [[File:Saho River, Daian-ji, Nara.jpg|thumb|Saho River flowing through Koi-no-Kubo in Nara City during autumn]] The river originates near {{ill|Kasugayama Primeval Forest|en|Kasugayama Primeval Forest|WD=Q5367406|lt=Mount Kasuga Primeval Forest|1=Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} between Mount Hana and Mount Yoshi, at {{ill|Ishikiri Pass|ja|石切峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}. It flows around Mount Wakakusa and traverses northern Nara City in a westward direction.<ref name="yamato" /><ref name="seto">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.seto.or.jp/upload/publish/seto_vol_82.pdf|title=Seto Inland Sea|publisher=Seto Inland Sea Environmental Conservation Association|page=66|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref> Near Nara City Hall, it turns southward, passing through Yamatokōriyama before merging with the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} (locally known as the {{ill|Hase River|ja|初瀬川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase River|1=Q17503129|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503129}}) near the Nara Prefecture Water Purification Center.<ref name="yamato" /> The river has a basin area of 129 km², covering approximately 12% of the Yamato River's basin, with a total length of 19 km.<ref name="seto" />
At its headwaters, the source features a waterfall known as Uguisu Falls, which is 10 meters in height.<ref name="yamato">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamato-shinkin.co.jp/news/detail/pdf/pdf_0059.pdf|title=Our Connection to the Yamato River No.9: Saho River|publisher=Yamato Shinkin Bank|accessdate=2023-03-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www3.pref.nara.jp/park/secure/2833/kasugayamagenshirin_guide.pdf|title=Guide to Kasuga Primeval Forest|publisher=Nara Prefecture|accessdate=2021-03-01}}</ref>
In ancient times, the Saho River was symbolically associated with the {{ill|Azure Dragon|ja|青龍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azure Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青竜|1=Azure Dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q515882}}, which flows to the east of the capital {{ill|Heijō-kyō|en|Heijō-kyō|WD=Q862509|lt=Heijō-kyō|1=Heijō-kyō}}, as per the principles of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}.<ref name="yamato" /> During the Edo period, the river was celebrated as part of the "Eight Views of Nanto" (南都八景), particularly for its fireflies.<ref name="yamato" />
The midstream section of the river in Nara City is lined with cherry trees on both banks, attracting crowds during the spring hanami season.<ref name="yamato" /> From late March to early April, the "Saho River Kawaji Cherry Blossom Festival" is held between the JR Saho River Bridge and the Funabashi shopping district. However, the traditional lantern-lit "Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival," which had been held for 23 years around the Ōmiya Bridge, ended in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20230228215824.html|title=End of the 23-Year Saho River Cherry Blossom Festival|publisher=Nara Newspaper|date=2023-03-01}}</ref>
== Municipalities along the River == ; Nara Prefecture : {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|WD=Q118213|lt=Yamatokōriyama|1=Yamatokōriyama}}
== Main Tributaries == * '''Iwai River''': Originates from {{ill|Mount Takamado|ja|高円山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takamado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} near {{ill|Byakugō-ji|en|Byakugō-ji|WD=Q1018450|lt=Byakugō-ji|1=Byakugō-ji}}, merges with Notogawa, and flows into the Saho River in {{ill|Hachijō|en|Hachijō|WD=Q401889|lt=Hachijō, Nara|1=Hachijō}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Akishino River''': Joins the Saho River in {{ill|Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja|観音寺, 大和郡山市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonji, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''{{ill|Bodaiji River|ja|菩提仙川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji River|1=Bodaisen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133257607}}''': Originates from {{ill|Shiroyama|en|Shiroyama|WD=Q7498962|lt=Shiroyama|1=Shiroyama}} in {{ill|Kitatsubao, Nara|ja|北椿尾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitatsubao, Nara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, flows through northern {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, and merges into the Saho River at {{ill|Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja|番条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banjō, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" /> * '''Takase River''': Originates near {{ill|Sakura Pass|ja|桜峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Pass|1=Q11537986|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11537986}} in {{ill|Fukuzumi, Tenri|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi, Tenri|1=Q11591777|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}}, and joins the Saho River at {{ill|Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja|伊豆七条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu, Yamatokōriyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="yamato" />
== Cultural Significance == Given its proximity to Nara's historic center, the Saho River has often been featured in Japanese poetry: * ''Treading on the small stones of Saho River / The black horse arrives, / Could it not be New Year already?'' ({{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|1=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume}}, ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}'') * ''Blocking the Saho River's flow / And planting rice paddies, / The farmer's swift meal—alone he eats.'' (Upper phrase: Nun / Lower phrase: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, ''Man'yōshū'') * ''Gazing across the Saho River's plain, / Caught in the breeze / Are the strands of green willow.'' ({{ill|Saigyō|en|Saigyō|WD=Q224689|lt=Saigyō|1=Saigyō}}, ''{{ill|Sankashū|en|Sankashū|WD=Q11467762|lt=Sankashū|1=Sankashū}}'')
